name: SetFit (Few-Shot Text Classification)

auth.notes: Local library; HuggingFace Hub token for model download

performance.notes: Training: minutes on GPU for 8-64 examples; inference: ~50ms per batch

agent_readiness.notes: Few-shot text classification framework from HuggingFace. Achieves strong performance with as few as 8-64 labeled examples per class. No need for large labeled datasets. Contrastive fine-tuning of sentence transformers. Works well for sentiment, topic classification, intent detection. Much cheaper than fine-tuning full LLMs. 3-step API: load model, train on few examples, predict. Supports multi-label and multi-class.

gotchas: Doesn't beat full fine-tuning with hundreds of examples
Quality depends heavily on base sentence transformer quality
Best for text classification — not generative tasks
Very large label spaces (1000+ classes) require more examples
best_when: Text classification with limited labeled data (8-64 examples/class)
Rapid intent classification for AI agents without LLM cost
Customer support ticket routing with minimal training data
Sentiment analysis or content moderation on a budget
avoid_when: When hundreds of labeled examples are available (full fine-tuning better)
Very large label spaces without sufficient examples
summary: SetFit is HuggingFace's few-shot text classification framework that achieves strong performance with just 8-64 labeled examples per class. Uses contrastive fine-tuning of sentence transformers. No large labeled datasets needed. Simple 3-step API. Best for rapid intent/sentiment/topic classification where labeled data is scarce. Much cheaper than LLM-based classification at inference time.
